items.title: KISS
items.desc: Write the simplest possible code. Prioritize readability and maintainability.

items.title: Anti-Feature Creep
items.desc: Implement only the requested functionality. Do not add speculative features.

items.title: Practical Safety
items.desc: Handle only expected, recoverable errors (missing resource, timeout, validation failure). Do not attempt to catch fatal errors (out of memory, stack overflow).

items.title: Surgical Precision
items.desc: Modify only the targeted code. Do not change adjacent logic or formatting.

items.title: Style Matching
items.desc: Follow existing naming, casing, and indentation strictly.

items.title: Respect Legacy
items.desc: Avoid refactoring working code unless directly required by task.

items.title: Dead Code
items.desc: Identify unused or unreachable code in comments, leave the implementation intact.

items.title: Deprecate, don't delete
items.desc: When code must be removed, mark it as deprecated (e.g., `@deprecated` comment) instead of deleting. Suggest alternatives.

items.title: Minimal Diff
items.desc: Fix the root cause with the smallest possible footprint to ease review.

items.title: Root Cause Analysis
items.desc: Provide a concise one-sentence explanation of the bug's root cause.

items.title: No New Dependencies
items.desc: Use standard library features; do not introduce new packages.

items.title: Side Effect Check
items.desc: Alert briefly if the fix might affect other parts of the system.

items.title: Test Suggestion
items.desc: Provide a minimal unit test (AAA pattern) that fails before the fix and passes after.

items.title: Critical Only
items.desc: Report only production-breaking bugs, security vulnerabilities, or severe logic flaws.

items.title: Standard Review
items.desc: Focus on correctness, edge cases, and performance; ignore minor style nits.

items.title: Pedantic Review
items.desc: Analyze naming, syntax, docs, and architectural alignment in detail.

items.title: Time Complexity
items.desc: Optimize for time complexity (prefer O(n) or O(log n)).

items.title: Resource Management
items.desc: Flag allocations >1MB, heavy I/O, or CPU loops >1000 iterations.

items.title: Concurrency
items.desc: Check for race conditions, thread-safety, and inefficient locking.

items.title: Asynchronous I/O
items.desc: Prefer non-blocking I/O (async patterns) for file, network, or database operations. Avoid blocking calls.

items.title: No Premature Optimization
items.desc: Do not optimize code unless it is on a critical path. If optimizing, suggest only changes with estimated >10% measurable improvement.

items.title: Modern Standards
items.desc: Favor latest stable language features that reduce boilerplate and improve safety.

items.title: Best Practices
items.desc: Apply design principles (modularity, single responsibility) only when code exceeds ~30 lines or is reused. For small/single-use code, prefer simplicity.

items.title: Security (Basic)
items.desc: Check for obvious flaws like hardcoded secrets or unsafe inputs.

items.title: Security (Standard)
items.desc: Verify against common vulnerabilities (OWASP) and ensure basic thread-safety.

items.title: Security (Audit)
items.desc: Deep data-flow analysis, formal encryption standards, and exhaustive concurrency checks.

items.title: High-level
items.desc: Explain purpose and main flow in 2-3 sentences. Skip implementation details.

items.title: Step-by-step
items.desc: Break down logic line by line with intermediate values where helpful.

items.title: Beginner-friendly
items.desc: Use analogies, define complex terms, avoid jargon.

items.title: Query Efficiency
items.desc: Detect N+1 problems, suggest batching/eager loading, and flag missing filters or indexes.

items.title: Resource Safety
items.desc: Favor language-standard idioms for automatic resource management (e.g., disposable patterns).

items.title: Injection Prevention
items.desc: Force parameterized queries; flag raw string concatenation in SQL or NoSQL commands.

items.title: Loose Coupling
items.desc: Replace hardcoded dependencies with abstractions (interfaces, callbacks) to allow mocking.

items.title: Functional Purity
items.desc: Favor pure functions where output depends only on input; flag hidden global state.

items.title: Test Scenarios
items.desc: Ensure logic can be easily tested for nulls, empty collections, and boundary values.

items.title: Structured Logging
items.desc: Use structured formats (e.g., JSON or key-value pairs). Include relevant context variables for easy filtering.

items.title: Telemetry & Tracing
items.desc: Add tracing spans and metrics for long-running or external operations to enable monitoring and debugging.

items.title: Exceptions
items.desc: Use standard language exceptions for flow control and error handling.

items.title: Result Pattern
items.desc: Avoid exceptions for expected failures. Return Result or Either types instead.

items.title: Mixed Strategy
items.desc: Use exceptions for unexpected/system failures; use return values (error codes, result types) for expected domain errors (e.g., validation, not found).

items.title: Silent
items.desc: Do not suggest structural changes. Fix logic within existing design.

items.title: Assistive
items.desc: Suggest structural patterns only if they fix major flaws or simplify logic.

items.title: Expert
items.desc: Actively recommend design patterns and structural improvements.

items.title: None
items.desc: No comments. Write self-documenting code with clear naming.

items.title: Summary
items.desc: Brief summaries for public classes/methods. Comments for 'why', not 'what'.

items.title: Detailed
items.desc: Full technical documentation with params, returns, exceptions, and 'why' comments.

items.title: Long Functions
items.desc: Warn if a function exceeds ~30 lines of non-comment code.

items.title: Deep Nesting
items.desc: Flag nesting depth > 3 levels (e.g., condition inside loop inside another condition).

items.title: Code Duplication
items.desc: Detect repeated blocks of code longer than 5-6 lines that could be extracted.

items.title: Privacy (GDPR)
items.desc: Flag any PII handling without masking, encryption, or secure disposal.

items.title: Safety (GPSR)
items.desc: Ensure fail-safe defaults and verify error logs do not leak sensitive internals.
